### Senior Data Engineer Gen AI at [FirstEnergy](https://www.linkedin.com/company/firstenergy-corp)
...
Built an Enterprise Data Lake to enable ML/GenAI workloads at scale, making data readily consumable for scientists and business stakeholders. Developed multi-tenant GenAI infrastructure using Amazon ECS + Fargate, supporting concurrent workloads across business units. Designed high-performance pipelines with AWS EMR, Glue, Redshift, Kinesis, and S3, reducing latency for LLM training/evaluation and real-time prompt generation. Leveraged Amazon SageMaker + Hugging Face to preprocess, tokenize, and fine-tune LLMs on domain-specific datasets. Deployed scalable APIs (API Gateway + Lambda) to serve GenAI responses directly into BI dashboards and enterprise applications. Automated data workflows with Apache Airflow, Nifi, and CI/CD pipelines (CodePipeline, Jenkins, CloudFormation), ensuring reliability and faster release cycles. Implemented advanced data governance and security frameworks on AWS (IAM, DynamoDB, Lambda) for fine-grained access control. Partnered with data science teams to accelerate predictive analytics and GenAI adoption, improving model deployment efficiency by 40%. Optimized Spark applications for large-scale data enrichment, streaming, and analytics, cutting pipeline runtime significantly. Enabled real-time personalization by integrating Kafka + Spark Streaming for sub-second insights. Show less

results.title: Preet G.
highlights:
Data Scientist at [FirstEnergy](https://www.linkedin.com/company/firstenergy-corp)
...
I am a results-driven Data Scientist with over eight years of experience in machine learning, predictive analytics, and computer vision, specializing in predictive modeling, deep learning, and geospatial analysis within the electricity transmission sector. Currently, I work on asset management analytics and predictive modeling for transmission systems, with a focus on drone-based inspections, criticality assessments, and predicting conductor health to enhance operational efficiency and infrastructure management. I am proficient in using Python, SQL, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, XGBoost, OpenCV, YOLO, and BERT, and I leverage Kedro pipelines for efficient productionalization. My technical expertise also includes data wrangling, feature engineering, and visualization using libraries such as NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, and Seaborn. I thrive in cross-functional teams, driving innovation through data-driven solutions and bridging the gap between data science and business strategy. I am always open to learning, collaborating, and exploring new opportunities in AI, data science.
...
[FirstEnergy
...
#### Data Scientist (Current)
...
- Develop and implement predictive models for transmission systems with a focus on asset management analytics, criticality assessments, and predicting conductor health to optimize maintenance processes and improve grid reliability.
...
- Lead computer vision initiatives by applying YOLO and other deep learning techniques to process drone footage, automating inspection for transmission lines.
...
- Apply Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques to build models for issue categorization from maintenance logs and operational data.
...
- Utilize machine learning algorithms such as Scikit-learn, XGBoost, and TensorFlow for predictive analytics, feature engineering, and model training across various grid management tasks.
...
- Work with Kedro pipelines to efficiently productionalize machine learning models, ensuring seamless integration with on-premise infrastructure while maintaining data security.
...
- Oversee the end-to-end machine learning lifecycle, including model development, testing, evaluation, deployment, and model performance monitoring to ensure continuous improvement. Key Projects:
...
- Developed and deployed a predictive model to assess the health of conductors, enabling more effective and timely maintenance, ultimately improving the reliability and lifespan of the transmission infrastructure.
...
- Led the development of a computer vision system using drone footage to automate the detection of defects and damage on transmission lines, reducing manual inspections and improving accuracy.
...
- Automated the criticality assessment process for transmission lines, enhancing decision-making speed and accuracy for maintenance prioritization.
...
- Created an NLP model to categorize maintenance issues, significantly improving operational efficiency by reducing manual effort and accelerating issue resolution. Show less

results.title: Freida Luckett
highlights:
Data Engineer IV at [FirstEnergy](https://www.linkedin.com/company/firstenergy-corp)
...
I am a senior level information technology professional with 20 years of experience in designing and delivering business solutions. I have built an extensive set of skills throughout my career, but my passion and expertise lies in the Microsoft SQL stack. I have spent the majority of my years as an IT professional in the area of Data Warehousing and Business Intelligence. As many of my positions have required me to split my time between development and analysis, I held the status of Certified Business Analysis Professional (CBAP) from 2012 to 2020. I have a natural ability to work well with others which has served me well when working towards project goals. I began my Business Intelligence training at Progressive Insurance writing ETL’s and reports. I then moved to Barnes Distribution where I sharpened my skills and added OLAP cube maintenance and design to the mix. As the Business Intelligence specialist at Covia, I led a SQL Server upgrade from 2008 to 2016, and completed a project to upgrade our Azure BI environment from ADFv1 to ADFv2. And in my role as Sr. Database Developer at Asurint, I implemented multiple data warehouses while also designing out of the box solutions for our extremely complex older systems. I have recently moved on to FirstEnergy where I expect to utilize a combination of my past experience and new skills yet to be learned to accomplish great things. results.title: Cybersecurity Automation Developer | Joblume
highlights: Energy at a Glance We are a forward-thinking electric utility powered by a diverse team of employees committed to making customers’ lives brighter, the environment better and our communities stronger. FirstEnergy (NYSE: FE) is dedicated to safety, reliability, and operational excellence. Headquartered in Akron, Ohio, FirstEnergy includes one of the nation's largest investor-owned electric systems, more than 24,500 miles of transmission lines that connect the Midwest and Mid-Atlantic regions, and a regulated generating fleet with a total capacity of 3,780 megawatts. This position is within FirstEnergy Service Co., a subsidiary of FirstEnergy Corp.  This position is in Akron, Ohio, reporting to the Supv of Threat Hunting, but has remote work opportunities while the person must be able to reach the FirstEnergy HQ facility based on business need within one hour travel time. This position may, subject to conditions and availability, qualify to be filled under the same terms but reporting to a regional office in Greensburg PA, Reading PA, Fairmont WV, Holmdel NJ, or Holland (Toledo) OH. The Cybersecurity Automation Developer is responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining automated solutions that scale and mature the organization’s security operations. This role enhances threat detection and accelerates incident response. A primary focus will be reducing manual workloads through automation, standardized playbooks, and repeatable engineering practices. This role designs and implements automation frameworks functions including alert triage, incident response and threat hunting. The Cybersecurity Automation Developer will develop and maintain playbooks and workflows within the SOAR, integrating tools including SIEM and EDR into cohesive automated pipelines. The Cybersecurity Automation Developer will lead initiatives to streamline security monitoring and remediation, create scalable scripting and orchestration solutions using Python, PowerShell, and JavaScript, and leverage APIs and data pipelines to normalize and correlate telemetry from multiple security sources. The Cybersecurity Automation Developer will analyze complex security datasets to identify gaps and opportunities, tune automated processes to reduce false positives, and support advanced detection through analytics and emerging technologies. Additionally, this position provides technical leadership by defining an automation strategy, mentoring junior developers, supporting vendor relationships, and contributing to architecture and lifecycle decisions. Strong communication skills, sound engineering judgment, and the ability to translate technical requirements into practical, scaled solutions are essential for success.  Responsibilities Include Design, develop, and maintain automated security workflows that streamline SOC and Threat Hunting operations, reduce manual effort, and accelerate incident detection, response, and remediation Lead expert-level development, tuning, and lifecycle management of SOAR playbooks to automate alert triage, enrichment, containment, and response processes Identify, troubleshoot, and remediate playbook failures, integration issues, and automation errors, performing break-fix and tuning activities prior to production deployment Architect and maintain integrations between SOAR, SIEM, EDR, and third-party security tools using APIs and custom scripting to ensure reliable data ingestion and orchestration Develop custom automation scripts and integrations in Python and JavaScript to extend platform capabilities and address unique SOC and enterprise security requirements Create and maintain dashboards, reports, and metrics to provide visibility into automation performance and effectiveness Install, validate, and deploy content packs and updates, following change management best practices to promote stable releases from development through production Serve as the primary subject matter expert (SME) for the SOAR and security automation

We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact.
